mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank rate hikes, increasing discount rates and reducing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when both GDPC1 and HOUST show growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 3.5% signals tight labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE falls below 3.5 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing activity, boosting corporate profits and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not significantly positive during periods of INDPRO growth (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in consumer credit (RSAFS) indicates reduced household spending, lowering corporate revenues and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS declines despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 150k signals a robust labor market, increasing consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when PAYEMS exceeds 150k (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals a manufacturing slowdown and labor market weakness, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when both INDPRO declines and UNRATE rises (t-test p > 0.05).
